title: Anchor bar and carriage stop assembly
abstract: A reformer has parallel tracks extending between a head end and a foot end and an anchor bar and carriage stop assembly fastened to the frame adjacent the foot end of the frame. The assembly includes an anchor bar slidably fastened to a pair of elongated plates each fastened to one of the tracks. Each plate has an elongated closed slot therein extending parallel to and below the one of the tracks. The anchor bar has opposite ends, each end extending through one of the elongated slots and through an elongated spacer member pivotally fastened to the end of the anchor bar. Each elongated spacer member has a distal end carrying a pin fastened thereto extending through the elongated slot, wherein the pin has a carriage stop fastened to the pin.
claims: 1. A reformer exercise apparatus comprising: a frame; a carriage on the frame configured for movement back and forth between a head end and a foot end of the frame on parallel tracks; one or more bias members fastened between the carriage and the foot end of the frame for biasing the carriage toward the foot end of the frame on the parallel tracks; a foot bar fastened at a foot end of the frame; and an anchor bar and carriage stop assembly fastened to the frame adjacent the foot end of the frame having an anchor bar slidably fastened to a pair of elongated plates each fastened to one of the tracks, each plate having an elongated closed slot therein extending parallel to and below the one of the tracks, wherein the anchor bar has opposite ends, each end extending through one of the elongated slots and through an elongated spacer member pivotally fastened to the end of the anchor bar, each elongated spacer member having a distal end carrying a pin fastened thereto extending through the elongated slot, wherein the pin has a carriage stop fastened to the pin.
